name: Mexico City
localTips: Tap or top up a Metro card for the subway — at about 5 pesos a ride it's the cheapest big-city transit on the continent, though avoid it at rush hour with luggage; use Uber or DiDi for late nights.
At 2,240 meters, the altitude hits harder than the heat does — go easy on alcohol the first day, drink more water than usual, and don't be surprised if stairs leave you winded.
Rainy season (roughly May–October) means a near-daily afternoon downpour around 3–6pm — mornings are usually clear, so front-load outdoor plans and keep an indoor backup for late afternoon.
Tipping is 10–15% at sit-down restaurants (check if 'propina' is already added to the bill) and 5–10 pesos for a taco stand is generous; rounding up for informal service is normal.
Never hail a taxi off the street — use Uber, DiDi or an app-booked 'taxi de sitio' from a taxi stand instead; it's the single most repeated safety tip locals give visitors.

activities.title: Zócalo & Metropolitan Cathedral
activities.description: One of the largest public squares on Earth, flanked by the Metropolitan Cathedral and the Palacio Nacional — a massive Mexican flag snaps overhead and the plaza never really empties.

activities.title: Casa de los Azulejos
activities.description: An 18th-century palace sheathed floor to roofline in blue-and-white Talavera tile, now a Sanborns café — walk in for free to see the Orozco mural over the grand staircase.

activities.title: Bosque de Chapultepec
activities.description: Latin America's largest city park — twice the size of Central Park, with lakes, jogging paths and the castle rising on its hill; free to wander, museums cluster along its edges.

activities.title: Chapultepec Zoo
activities.description: A free, century-old zoo inside the park famous for its giant pandas — one of the few places outside China with a breeding pair, alongside jaguars and Mexican gray wolves.

activities.title: Coyoacán plaza & market
activities.description: Cobblestone streets, mariachis in the twin plazas, and a covered market of leather goods and food stalls — the bohemian village where Frida Kahlo and Leon Trotsky both lived.

activities.title: Parque México & Condesa streets
activities.description: An Art Deco park at the center of Condesa's tree-lined, café-filled streets — locals doing tai chi at dawn, dog walkers all day, and outdoor tables spilling out by evening.

activities.title: Templo Mayor ruins, free street view
activities.description: The excavated foundations of the main Aztec temple, uncovered by accident by electrical workers in 1978, sit right off the Zócalo — the outdoor ruin field is visible without a museum ticket.

activities.title: Mercado de San Juan
activities.description: A gourmet market of exotic meats and rare cheeses that supplies the city's top chefs — free to wander the aisles and graze on tastings from vendors who've run stalls for generations.

activities.title: Kiosco Morisco, Santa María la Ribera
activities.description: A cast-iron Moorish-style pavilion built for the 1884 World's Fair and shipped here piece by piece — the quiet centerpiece of one of the city's most overlooked historic neighborhoods.

activities.title: Museo Soumaya
activities.description: Carlos Slim's free museum inside a rippling, silver-tiled Fernando Romero building in Polanco — Rodin's largest collection outside France, plus five centuries of European painting, no charge.

activities.title: Bazar Sábado, San Ángel
activities.description: San Ángel's cobblestone Plaza San Jacinto fills with painters' easels and craft stalls every Saturday — free to browse a genuine artists' market in one of the city's prettiest colonial corners.

activities.title: Alameda Central
activities.description: The oldest public park in the Americas (1592), a shaded grid of fountains and marble statues facing the Palacio de Bellas Artes' Art Nouveau dome — the city's original front lawn.

activities.title: Basílica de Guadalupe
activities.description: The most-visited Catholic shrine in the world after the Vatican — a modern circular basilica holding the 1531 image of the Virgin, viewed from moving walkways beneath the altar, free to enter.

activities.title: Tacos al pastor off the trompo
activities.description: Thin slices carved straight off a spinning, pineapple-topped spit onto warm corn tortillas at a stand like El Huequito or Los Cocuyos — Mexico City's defining late-night meal.
activities.insiderNote: Order 'con todo' for onion, cilantro and pineapple, and ask for the al pastor 'de la orilla' (from the crispy outer edge) — regulars know to ask for it specifically.

activities.title: Pulque tasting at a traditional pulquería
activities.description: Pulque is a fermented agave drink older than tequila, served in curado (fruit-flavored) or natural at old-school pulquerías like Los Insurgentes — Aztec-era drinking, still going.
activities.insiderNote: Order a sampler flight of curados (guava, celery, oatmeal are classics) before committing to a full jarra — natural pulque is an acquired, tangy taste.

activities.title: Mezcal flight at La Clandestina
activities.description: A dim, brick-walled Roma bar pouring small-batch mezcal from named producers across Oaxaca — order a flight with orange slices and worm salt, the traditional way to taste it.
activities.insiderNote: Ask the bartender which mezcal is the 'joven' (young, unaged) house pour that week — the lineup rotates and staff genuinely love walking you through it.

activities.title: La Ópera Bar's bullet hole
activities.description: An 1876 cantina with gilded ceilings and mirrored walls where — legend says — Pancho Villa fired his pistol into the ceiling; the bullet hole is still pointed out today by staff.
activities.insiderNote: Ask your server to point out the actual bullet hole above the bar — it's small and easy to miss unless someone shows you exactly where to look.

activities.title: Tostada counter at Mercado de Coyoacán
activities.description: A row of competing tostada stalls inside Coyoacán's covered market piles ceviche, tinga and cochinita pibil onto crisp fried tortillas — locals debate which counter is best for decades.
activities.insiderNote: Stall 'Tostadas Coyoacán' is the famous one with the line, but the smaller stands beside it are just as good with zero wait — order two tostadas and a jamaica water.

activities.title: Mercado de la Merced
activities.description: The city's original, sprawling wholesale market — mountains of chiles, whole roasted pigs and a maze of alleys selling everything from piñatas to live animals; overwhelming and completely real.
activities.insiderNote: Go with cash in small bills on a weekday morning, keep bags zipped and don't wander the outer edges after dark — this is a working market, not a tourist stop, and it shows.

activities.title: Unmarked door at Hanky Panky
activities.description: Enter through a fake taco stand's walk-in fridge into a Prohibition-styled cocktail bar with bartenders in vintage dress — one of the city's original hidden-door speakeasies.
activities.insiderNote: The taco counter out front is a real, functioning decoy — ask quietly for a table and staff will walk you through the fridge door. Reserve ahead on weekends.

activities.title: Churros con chocolate at El Moro
activities.description: Frying churros around the clock since 1935, this Centro institution pairs sugar-dusted churros with thick drinking chocolate — the after-midnight ritual for generations of Chilangos.
activities.insiderNote: Order the chocolate 'espesa' (thick, Mexican-style) for dipping rather than the thinner version, and go after 1am if you want the real night-owl crowd.

activities.title: Sunday Ciclotón on Paseo de la Reforma
activities.description: Every Sunday morning the city closes 32km of major avenues, including Reforma, to cars — locals fill it with bikes, skates and strollers past the Angel of Independence, car-free.
activities.insiderNote: Rent a bike from an Ecobici station (day passes available to visitors) and ride from Chapultepec to the Zócalo before 11am, when the route reopens to traffic.

activities.title: Late set at Zinco Jazz Club
activities.description: A basement jazz club inside a former 1930s bank vault downtown — exposed brick, low light and serious local and touring jazz acts most nights of the week.
activities.insiderNote: The entrance is unmarked next to a bank building on Motolinía street — look for a small doorman rather than a sign. Reserve a table for the second set, it's less crowded.

activities.title: Museo Nacional de Antropología
activities.description: The Aztec Sun Stone, Mayan jade masks and a rainfall 'umbrella' fountain over the central courtyard — one of the world's great archaeology museums, worth a full morning.
activities.bookingQuery: Museo Nacional de Antropologia guided tour Mexico City

activities.title: Palacio de Bellas Artes murals
activities.description: Diego Rivera, Orozco and Siqueiros murals fill the upper floors of this Art Nouveau-meets-Art Deco marble palace — book ahead if you want to catch a Ballet Folklórico performance too.
activities.bookingQuery: Palacio de Bellas Artes murals and Ballet Folklorico ticket

activities.title: Frida Kahlo Museum (Casa Azul)
activities.description: The cobalt-blue house where Kahlo was born, lived and died — her studio, wheelchair and Diego Rivera's ashes upstairs. Timed tickets sell out days ahead.
activities.bookingQuery: Frida Kahlo Museum Casa Azul timed ticket Mexico City

activities.title: Chapultepec Castle
activities.description: The only royal castle in the Americas, home to Emperor Maximilian — stained glass, a hilltop terrace over the whole valley, and the National History Museum inside.
activities.bookingQuery: Chapultepec Castle entry ticket Mexico City

activities.title: Teotihuacan pyramids day trip
activities.description: Climb the Pyramid of the Sun, the third-largest pyramid on Earth, at a 2,000-year-old city whose builders remain unknown even to archaeologists — an hour outside town.
activities.bookingQuery: Teotihuacan pyramids day trip from Mexico City

activities.title: Xochimilco trajinera boat ride
activities.description: Flat-bottomed, flower-painted gondolas punt through the last remnant of the Aztec chinampa canal system — mariachi boats and food-vendor boats pull up alongside you as you float.
activities.bookingQuery: Xochimilco trajinera boat tour Mexico City

activities.title: Templo Mayor Museum
activities.description: The site museum built directly over the excavated Aztec temple — a massive carved Coyolxauhqui stone disc and thousands of ritual offerings dug from beneath the modern city streets.
activities.bookingQuery: Templo Mayor Museum entry ticket Mexico City

activities.title: Lucha libre at Arena México
activities.description: Masked wrestlers fly off the ropes at the 'cathedral of lucha libre' — beer vendors, chanting crowds and technicolor masks in a Friday-night spectacle unlike anything else.
activities.bookingQuery: lucha libre Arena Mexico wrestling tickets

activities.title: Casa Luis Barragán guided tour
activities.description: The Pritzker Prize-winning architect's own house — pink and yellow walls, shafts of colored light — a UNESCO site visitable only by advance-booked guided tour, small groups only.
activities.bookingQuery: Casa Luis Barragan guided tour ticket Mexico City

activities.title: Hot air balloon over Teotihuacan
activities.description: Lift off before dawn to drift silently over the Pyramids of the Sun and Moon as the desert valley turns gold — a bucket-list sunrise, weather-dependent and unforgettable when it flies.
activities.bookingQuery: hot air balloon ride Teotihuacan pyramids

activities.title: Roma & Condesa food tour
activities.description: A guided crawl through Mexico City's trendiest neighborhoods — market stalls, taquerías and a mezcal stop, with a local explaining what's actually on your plate.
activities.bookingQuery: Roma Condesa food tour Mexico City

activities.title: Torre Latinoamericana observation deck
activities.description: A 1956 skyscraper engineered to survive earthquakes, with a 44th-floor deck delivering the best 360° view over the Centro Histórico and the volcanoes beyond on a clear day.
activities.bookingQuery: Torre Latinoamericana observation deck ticket Mexico City
